headlines.text: US gas hits $4/gallon for first time since 2022 as Iran war chokes Strait of Hormuz
Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Iran's power plants, oil wells, and desalination plants if no deal
Israel and US launch new wave of strikes on Isfahan; fireball caught on video
Iran's parliament approves plan to charge a toll on Strait of Hormuz shipping
Stocks rally ~1% on WSJ report Trump willing to end war even if Hormuz stays closed
Hegseth refuses to rule out US ground troops in Iran at Pentagon briefing
Supreme Court sides with Christian therapist, strikes down Colorado conversion therapy ban
Tiger Woods DUI arrest affidavit released: bloodshot eyes, dilated pupils, failed sobriety tests
DHS shutdown enters record territory as Congress goes on Easter recess with no deal
Pakistan and China release five-point peace plan to end Iran war, call for immediate ceasefire

breaking.text: Hegseth at Pentagon briefing: US will NOT foreclose ground troops in Iran — 'There are 15 different ways we could come at them with boots on the ground'

stories.hed: Trump Threatens Obliteration, Then Floats Quitting — Iran Says 'Come On In'
stories.dek: Trump threatened to destroy Iran's power plants and water supply if no deal, then reportedly told aides he'd end the war even with Hormuz still shut. Iran responded by saying it is 'waiting' for US ground troops so it can 'set them on fire,' which is a very cool and normal diplomatic communique.

stories.hed: Houthis Enter the War, Threatening to Block a Second Chokepoint at Bab al-Mandeb
stories.dek: Yemen's Houthis have begun launching strikes on Israel, and analysts warn they could now blockade Bab al-Mandeb, giving the world two strategic waterways to panic about simultaneously. Oil tanker captains are reportedly considering careers in agri-food logistics.

stories.hed: Supreme Court Strikes Down Colorado Conversion Therapy Ban, Calling Therapist Speech Protected
stories.dek: The Supreme Court ruled in favor of a Christian therapist who argued Colorado's ban on conversion therapy for minors violated her First Amendment rights. The decision sets precedent that therapist-patient conversations are constitutionally protected speech, with broad implications for LGBTQ+ youth protections nationwide.

stories.hed: DHS Shutdown Hits Record 44+ Days as Congress Skips Town for Easter
stories.dek: The Department of Homeland Security has been without funding for over 44 days — the longest shutdown of a federal agency in US history — while both chambers headed out on a two-week Easter recess without a deal. TSA agents are working without pay and airport lines are at historic lengths, which is everyone's problem except apparently Congress's.

stories.hed: Jon Stewart to Trump: 'Can You Imagine Any Other Wartime President Being This F---ing Indulgent?'
stories.dek: Jon Stewart went after Trump's conduct during the Iran war, saying his leering, distracted behavior is less 'commander-in-chief' and more 'grandpa who lost his filter in public.' Meanwhile Trump was busy posting about renaming a Florida airport after himself and pitching a Miami skyscraper, which tracks.

markets.context: Stocks surged ~1% on a WSJ report that Trump told aides he is willing to end the Iran war even if the Strait of Hormuz stays largely closed, igniting relief buying across all three major indexes.

gas.note: Your wallet is on fire — conveniently, so is most of Iran.

popCulture.hed: Tiger Woods DUI Arrest Affidavit Released: Prescription Painkillers, Failed Sobriety Tests, Bloodshot Eyes
popCulture.dek: Details from the March 27 arrest affidavit dropped today, showing Woods had extremely dilated pupils and failed field sobriety tests after his Range Rover rollover in Jupiter Island, FL. The 50-year-old blew 0.00 on the breathalyzer but refused a urine test — a now-criminal offense under Florida's new 'Trenton's Law' — putting his Masters hopes and his freedom in the same uncertain fairway.

genZ.definition: A viral TikTok character born from the 'Italian brainrot' meme wave — specifically an Indonesian wooden plank creature that appears at the worst possible moments to derail whatever you are doing. It exploded across FYPs in March 2026 as a catch-all metaphor for chaotic interruptions you cannot predict or stop.
genZ.usage: You're trying to negotiate a peace deal with Iran, great progress is being made, and then Tung Tung Tung Sahur shows up and Iran's parliament votes to install a toll booth in the Strait of Hormuz.

tldr: The US-Israel war on Iran entered day 32 with strikes on Isfahan, gas hitting $4 a gallon, and Trump simultaneously threatening to obliterate Iran's infrastructure and telling aides he might just call the whole thing off. Markets rallied on the latter, everyone else just stared at the pump.
